title: Initial Readings, Meter Energy

title: Test sensors ready
question:
Fit Testo Temperature sensors to:
1) the DHW Output from the HIU
2) the kitchen tap.
notes: Use a clamp-on probe on the HIU output, and ensure the tap sensor is retained or held in tap water throughout the following stages.

title: DHW pipework cold prior to response time testing.
question: Isolate the primary supply to the HIU, run the kitchen HOT tap for 3 minutes UNTIL COLD, and record the final tap temperature.
notes: Re-open the primary supplies when completed. The time may be shortened if temperature has settled to extected values.

title: Time for Kitchen Tap to reach 45°C
question:
Start the kitchen tap at the same time as a stopwatch.

Time how long it takes for the tap temperature to reach 45°C?
notes: A stopwatch and temperature probe are required for this step.
failtext: It takes the kitchen tap {{tapTime45C}} seconds to reach 45°C, longer than the required 45s.

title: DHW Response
question: Did the tap achieve 45C within 45 seconds?

title: Kitchen Tap Temperature
question: What is the temperature of the kitchen tap after 3 minutes?
notes: A temperature probe is required for this step. Leave tap running for next stages. The target temperature is {{tSetpointDHW}}°C.

title: Adjusted Kitchen Tap Temperature
question: Please adjust the DHW temperature setting in the HIU until the tap temperature is {{tSetpointDHW}}°C +/- {{tTolerance}}°C. Adjust previous answer accordingly.
notes: Leave tap running for next stages.

title: Peak HTG Primary Flow Rate (litres per hour)
question: What is the Peak Primary Flow Rate the AVPL is set for?

title: Run Central Heating
question: Turn on heating or open primary HTG valve.
notes: If the red clip is fitted, or aactuator head removed, then there is no need to power up heating.

title: DPCV Adjusted to Design Flowrate
question: Adjust the DPCV valve from minimum setting until the heat meter displays a flow rate of at least {{m3hHTGDesign}} m3/h. Record the flow rate in the comment box.

title: TRVs Adjusted
question: Are all TRVs adjusted to design?
options: [{"title":"Yes","value":"Yes"},{"title":"No","value":"No"},{"title":"Not Checked","value":"Not Checked"},{"title":"No Design Data","value":"No Design Data"}]

title: Check each room stat controls the correct zones.
question: Check each room stat controls the correct zones.
notes: This may take a few minutes.  Start will all zones turned off. One zone at a time, locate the appropriate room thermostat and increase temperature until there is demand.

title: Run Central Heating
question: Turn up all room thermostats and TRVs to maximum.
notes: This is to simulate peak load.

title: Pump Speed
question: Check that the heating pump starts and enter the speed setting below.
notes: Please include any additional settings such as Proportional or Fixed Head, and any units.

title: Flow to all UFH zones
question: Check that all the underfloor heating manifold actuators open.
notes: With all thermostats calling there should be flow to all zones.

title: Underfloor Heating Zone Flow Rates
question: Please record the flow rates for each UFH Zone.
notes: Ensure that they are set to design requirements as per drawing {{ufhLayouts}}.

title: Radiator Return Temperatures
question: Record the flow and return temperatures and TRV pre-settings for each radiator below.
notes: Add, edit and copy down lines as with a normal spreadsheet. Right click for further actions including deleting lines.

title: UFH Manual Override
question: Record below any known causes for zones not opening. To continue heating tests, manually loosen the manifold actuators to introduce flow.
notes: Please add detail. Do not fully remove actuators if they are not clearly labelled.
